title: Java Module Services
description: Declare module service consumers and providers with uses and provides directives.
learningGoals: Use uses to declare that a module consumes a service through ServiceLoader.
Use provides ... with to declare one or more provider implementation classes.

completionChecks.prompt: Which directive says this module consumes Reporter services?
completionChecks.explanation: The uses directive names the service interface that this module may load as a consumer. It does not export packages or declare a dependency by itself.

completionChecks.prompt: What does provides com.example.spi.Reporter with com.example.impl.JsonReporter mean?
choices: JsonReporter implements the Reporter service for service consumers
Reporter is exported only to JsonReporter
The service is an automatic module
completionChecks.explanation: provides ... with registers provider implementation classes for the named service interface. The provider class does not need its package exported for service loading.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if JsonReporter does not implement com.example.spi.Reporter?
choices: The module still compiles but ServiceLoader skips JsonReporter
The module declaration fails because the provider must implement the service type
The uses directive automatically adapts JsonReporter
The exports directive makes JsonReporter a provider
completionChecks.explanation: A provides directive is checked against the service contract. The provider class named after with must be a valid implementation of the service interface.
